questions.question_text: The area enclosed by the ellipse {tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{a^2}}} + \frac{{{y^2}}}{{{b^2}}} = 1{/tex} is equal to
questions.question_html: The area enclosed by the ellipse {tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{a^2}}} + \frac{{{y^2}}}{{{b^2}}} = 1{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation: Area of standard ellipse is given by : {tex}\pi a b.{/tex}

questions.question_text: The area of the loop between the curve y = a sin x and the x – axis and x= 0 , {tex}x = \pi .{/tex} is
questions.question_html: The area of the loop between the curve y = a sin x and the x – axis and x= 0 , {tex}x = \pi .{/tex} is
questions.explanation: Required area = {tex}\int\limits_0^\pi {a\operatorname{sinx} } dx {/tex} {tex} = a\left[ { - \cos x} \right]_0^\pi {/tex} {tex} = a( - \cos \pi + \cos 0) = a(1 + 1) = 2a{/tex}

questions.question_text: The area enclosed between the curves y = x 3 , x- axis and two ordinates x = 1 to x = 2 is [in square units]
questions.question_html: The area enclosed between the curves y = x3, x- axis and two ordinates x = 1 to x = 2 is [in square units]
questions.explanation: Required area: = {tex}\int\limits_1^2 {y.dx} {/tex} = {tex}∫_1^2x^3 dx{/tex} = {tex}[\frac{x^4}{4}]_1^2{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2^4}{4}-\frac{1^4}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{16}{4}-\frac{1}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{15}{4}{/tex} sq units

questions.question_text: Area of the region bounded by the curves y = e x , x = a , x = b and the x- axis is given by
questions.question_html: Area of the region bounded by the curves y = ex, x = a , x = b and the x- axis is given by
questions.explanation: Required area = {tex}\int\limits_a^b {{e^x}dx} = \left[ {{e^x}} \right]_a^b = {e^b} - {e^a}{/tex}

questions.question_text: AOB is the positive quadrant of the ellipse {tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{a^2}}} + \frac{{{y^2}}}{{{b^2}}} = 1{/tex} in which OA = a and OB = b. The area between the arc AB and chord AB of the ellipse is
questions.question_html: AOB is the positive quadrant of the ellipse {tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{{{a^2}}} + \frac{{{y^2}}}{{{b^2}}} = 1{/tex} in which OA = a and OB = b. The area between the arc AB and chord AB of the ellipse is
questions.explanation: Required area := {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} area of ellipse – area of right angled triangle AOB. = {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} {tex}\pi {/tex} ab – {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} ab = {tex}\frac{{ab}}{4}{/tex} ( {tex}\pi {/tex} - 2 ) .

questions.question_text: Area bounded by the curve y = x 3 , the x-axis and the ordinates x = -2 and x = 1 is
questions.question_html: Area bounded by the curve y = x3, the x-axis and the ordinates x = -2 and x = 1 is
questions.explanation: Required area {tex}\int\limits_{ - 2}^1 {{x^3}dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_{ - 2}^0 {{x^3}dx}{/tex} + {tex}\int\limits_{ 0}^1 {{x^3}dx}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\frac{x^4}{4}\right]_{-2}^0{/tex} + {tex}\left.[\frac{x^4}{4}]\right._{0}^1{/tex} = {tex}\left[0-\frac{(-2)^4}{4}\right]{/tex} + {tex}\left[\frac{1}{4}-0\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{16}{4}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{17}{4}{/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curve y = x 3 , the x – axis and two ordinates x = 1 and x = 2 is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curve y = x3, the x – axis and two ordinates x = 1 and x = 2 is
questions.explanation: {tex}\int\limits_1^2 {\left| y \right|dx} = \int\limits_1^2 {{{\left| x \right|}^3}dx = \int\limits_1^2 {{x^3}dx} = \left[ {\frac{{{x^4}}}{4}} \right]} _1^2 = \left[ {\frac{{16}}{4} - \frac{1}{4}} \right] = \frac{{15}}{4}{/tex} sq.units.

questions.question_text: If A is the area between the curve y = sin2x , x – axis and the lines x = {tex}\frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} and x = {tex}\frac{{3\pi }}{4}{/tex} is :
questions.question_html: If A is the area between the curve y = sin2x , x – axis and the lines x = {tex}\frac{\pi }{4}{/tex} and x = {tex}\frac{{3\pi }}{4}{/tex} is :
questions.explanation: Required area : = {tex} \int\limits_{\frac{\pi }{4}}^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {ydx} + \int\limits_{\frac{\pi }{2}}^{\frac{{3\pi }}{4}} { - ydx} \\{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_{\frac{\pi }{4}}^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\sin 2x.dx} - \int\limits_{\frac{\pi }{2}}^{\frac{{3\pi }}{4}} {\sin 2x.dx} \\{/tex} = {tex}\left[ {\frac{{ - \cos 2x}}{2}} \right]_{\frac{\pi }{4}}^{\frac{\pi }{2}} + \left[ {\frac{{\cos 2x}}{2}} \right]_{\frac{\pi }{2}}^{\frac{{3\pi }}{4}} \\ {/tex} = {tex} \frac{1}{2} + \frac{1}{2} {/tex} = 1

questions.question_text: The area bounded by y = 2cosx , x = 0 to x = 2 {tex}\pi{/tex} and the axis of x in square units is -
questions.question_html: The area bounded by y = 2cosx , x = 0 to x = 2{tex}\pi{/tex} and the axis of x in square units is -
questions.explanation: Required area: = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {ydx} \\ {/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {2\cos xdx} \\ {/tex} = {tex} \left[ {2\sin x} \right]_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {/tex} = 2 Therefore, total area from x = 0 to x = 2 {tex}\pi {/tex} is 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 8 sq. units.

questions.question_text: The area of the figure bounded by the curve y = log e x , the x – axis and the straight line x = e is
questions.question_html: The area of the figure bounded by the curve y = logex , the x – axis and the straight line x = e is
questions.explanation: Required area : {tex} \int\limits_1^e {{{\log }_e}xdx} \\ {/tex} = {tex}\left[ {x\log x - x} \right]_1^e \\{/tex} = {tex} (e\log e - e){/tex} - {tex}(1\log 1 - 1) {/tex} = 1

questions.question_text: The area enclosed by the curve {tex}y = 2\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} ,x \in \left[ {0,1} \right]{/tex} is
questions.question_html: The area enclosed by the curve {tex}y = 2\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} ,x \in \left[ {0,1} \right]{/tex} is
questions.explanation: Required area : = {tex}\int\limits_0^1 {ydx} {/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^1 {2\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} dx} \\ {/tex} = {tex} 2\left[ {\frac{{x\sqrt {1 - {x^2}} }}{2} + \frac{1}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}x} \right]_0^1 \\ {/tex} = {tex}2(\frac{1}{2}{\sin ^{ - 1}}1) {/tex} = {tex} \frac{\pi }{2} \\ {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area of the region between the curve y = 4 –x 2 , {tex}0 \leqslant x \leqslant 3{/tex} and the x –axis is equal to
questions.question_html: The area of the region between the curve y = 4 –x2 , {tex}0 \leqslant x \leqslant 3{/tex} and the x –axis is equal to
questions.explanation: y = {tex}4 - {x^2} > 0 \Rightarrow - 2 < x < 2 \\{/tex} And {tex}y = 4 - {x^2} < 0 \Rightarrow x < - 2 {/tex} or x > 2 Required area: = {tex}\int\limits_0^2 {(4 - {x^2})dx} + \left| {\int\limits_2^3 {(4 - {x^2})} dx} \right| \\ {/tex} = {tex}\frac{{16}}{3} + \left| { - \frac{7}{3}} \right| \\{/tex} = {tex} \frac{{23}}{3}{/tex} sq.units

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curve |x|+ y = 1 and the x-axis is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curve |x|+ y = 1 and the x-axis is
questions.explanation: The given curve consists of two straight lines x + y = 1 (x ≥ 0) and -x + y = 1 (x < 0) Required area: = {tex}2\int\limits_0^1 {ydx}{/tex} = {tex}2\int\limits_0^1 {(1 - \left| x \right|} )dx \\ {/tex} = {tex} 2\int\limits_0^1 {(1 - x)dx} {/tex} = {tex} 2\left[ {x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_0^1 \\{/tex} = 1 sq.unit

questions.question_text: The area of the region bounded by the ellipse {tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{{25}} + \frac{{{y^2}}}{{16}} = 1{/tex} is
questions.question_html: The area of the region bounded by the ellipse {tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{{25}} + \frac{{{y^2}}}{{16}} = 1{/tex}is
questions.explanation: The area of the standard ellipse is given by ; {tex}\pi a b.{/tex} Here, a = 5 and b = 4 Therefore, the area of curve is {tex}\pi \left( 5 \right)\left( 4 \right) = 20\pi .{/tex}

questions.question_text: The area of the curve x 2 + y 2 = 2ax is
questions.question_html: The area of the curve x2 + y2 = 2ax is
questions.explanation: Given equation is of a circle; (x - a) 2 + y 2 = a 2 Its radius is equal to a. Therefore required area is = {tex}\pi {a^2}.{/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curve y = 4x - x 2 and the x- axis is equal to
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curve y = 4x - x2 and the x- axis is equal to
questions.explanation: For x – axis , y = 0, Therefore, {tex}4x{\text{ }}-{\text{ }}{x^2} = {\text{ }}0\;{/tex} Therefore , x = 0 or x = 4. Required area : {tex}\int\limits_0^4 {y.dx}{/tex} = {tex}\int\limits_0^4 {(4x - {x^2}).dx} \\ {/tex} = {tex}\left[ {2{x^2} - \frac{{{x^3}}}{3}} \right]_0^4{/tex} = 32 - {tex}\frac{{64}}{3}{/tex} - 0 = {tex}\frac{{32}}{3} \\ {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curve y 2 = x, line y = 4 and y – axis is equal to
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curve y2= x, line y = 4 and y – axis is equal to
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{{64}}{3}{/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by y = log x , the x – axis and the ordinates x = 1 and x = 2 is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by y = log x , the x – axis and the ordinates x = 1 and x = 2 is
questions.explanation: Required area: = {tex}\int\limits_1^2 {y.dx}{/tex} = {tex} \int\limits_1^2 {\log xdx} {/tex} = {tex} \left[ {x\log x-x} \right]_1^2 {/tex} = 2 log 2 - 2 - log 1 + 1 = 2 log 2 - 1 = 2 log 2 - log e = log 4 - log e = {tex}\log \left( {\frac{4}{e}} \right) {/tex}

questions.question_text: Area lying in the first quadrant and bounded by the circle x 2 + y 2 = 4 and the line x = y {tex}\sqrt 3 {/tex} is
questions.question_html: Area lying in the first quadrant and bounded by the circle x2 + y2 = 4 and the line x = y{tex}\sqrt 3 {/tex} is
questions.explanation: The given circle is x 2 + y 2 = 4….(1) Its centre is (0,0) and radius = 2. The given line is x = {tex}\sqrt 3 {/tex} y ……….(2) Therefore, we have When y = 1, x = {tex}\sqrt 3 {/tex} . The required area is: {tex}\int\limits_0^{\sqrt 3 } {\frac{x}{{\sqrt 3 }}dx} + \int\limits_{\sqrt 3 }^2 {\sqrt {4 - {x^2}} dx} \\{/tex} = {tex} \frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}\left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_0^{\sqrt 3 } \\ {/tex} + {tex}\left[ {\frac{{x\sqrt {4 - {x^2}} }}{2} + \frac{4}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{x}{2}} \right)} \right]_{\sqrt 3 }^2 \\{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\pi }{3} \\{/tex}

questions.question_text: The area of the circle x 2 + y 2 = 16 exterior to the parabola y 2 = 6x is
questions.question_html: The area of the circle x2 + y2 = 16 exterior to the parabola y2 = 6x is

questions.question_text: Area bounded by the curves satisfying the conditions {tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{{25}} + \frac{{{y^2}}}{{36}} \leqslant 1 \leqslant \frac{x}{5} + \frac{y}{6}{/tex} is given by
questions.question_html: Area bounded by the curves satisfying the conditions {tex}\frac{{{x^2}}}{{25}} + \frac{{{y^2}}}{{36}} \leqslant 1 \leqslant \frac{x}{5} + \frac{y}{6}{/tex} is given by

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the parabola y = x 2 + 1 and the straight line x + y = 3 is given by
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the parabola y = x2 + 1 and the straight line x + y = 3 is given by
questions.explanation: The two curves parabola and the line meet where, 3 - x = {tex} {x^2} + 1 \\{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {x^2} + x - 2{/tex} = 0 {tex}\\ \Rightarrow x = - 2,1. \\ {/tex} Required area ; {tex} \int\limits_{ - 2}^1 {\{ 3 - x - ({x^2} + 1)\} dx} \\{/tex} = {tex}\left[ {2x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - \frac{{{x^3}}}{3}} \right]_{ - 2}^1 \\ {/tex} = {tex}\frac{9}{2} \\ {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curves {tex}{y^2}{/tex} = x andy = {tex}{x^2}{/tex} is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curves {tex}{y^2}{/tex} = x andy = {tex}{x^2}{/tex} is
questions.explanation: The two curves meet in ( 0 , 0 ) and ( 1, 1 ).The required area lies above the curve y = x 2 and below x = y 2 and is equal to : {tex}\int\limits_0^1 {\left( {\sqrt x - {x^2}} \right)dx} = \left[ {\frac{2}{3}{x^{\frac{3}{2}}} - \frac{{{x^3}}}{3}} \right]_0^1 = \frac{2}{3} - \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{3}{/tex}

questions.question_text: The area common to the circle x 2 + y 2 = 16a 2 and the parabola y 2 = 6ax is
questions.question_html: The area common to the circle x2 + y2 = 16a2 and the parabola y2 = 6ax is
questions.explanation: Required area: = {tex}2\left[ {\int\limits_0^{2a} {\sqrt {6ax} dx + \int\limits_{2a}^{4a} {\sqrt {16{a^2} - {x^2}} dx} } } \right] \\ {/tex} = {tex}2\sqrt{6a}\;\left[\frac{x^{\displaystyle\frac32}}{\displaystyle\frac32}\right]_0^{2a}+2\;\left[\frac{x\sqrt{16a^2-x^2\;}}2+\frac{16a^2}2\sin^{-1}\frac x{4a}\right]_{2a}^{4a}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4a^2}3\left(4\mathrm\pi+\sqrt3\right){/tex}

questions.question_text: The area of the region bounded by the curves y = {tex}\left| {x - 1} \right|{/tex} and y = 3 - {tex}\left| x \right|{/tex} is
questions.question_html: The area of the region bounded by the curves y = {tex}\left| {x - 1} \right|{/tex} and y = 3 - {tex}\left| x \right|{/tex} is
questions.explanation: Required area: = {tex} \int\limits_{ - 1}^{ - 3} {(3 - \left| x \right| - \left| {x - 1} \right|)dx} {/tex} = {tex}\left[ {3x - \frac{{x\left| x \right|}}{2} - \frac{{(x - 1)\left| {x - 1} \right|}}{2}} \right]_{ - 1}^2 {/tex} = {tex}\left( {2 - 2 - \frac{1}{2}} \right) - \left( { - 3 + \frac{1}{2} + 2} \right){/tex} = 4

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curves y = {tex}\left| x \right|{/tex} - 1 and y = - {tex}\left| x \right|{/tex} + 1 is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curves y = {tex}\left| x \right|{/tex} - 1 and y = -{tex}\left| x \right|{/tex} + 1 is
questions.explanation: The graph of modulus function is V-shaped graph. Therefore , from graph , the area is = {tex}\sqrt 2 \times \sqrt 2 = 2.{/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the parabola {tex}{y^2}{/tex} = x and the straight line 2y = x is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the parabola {tex}{y^2}{/tex} = x and the straight line 2y = x is
questions.explanation: Required area : = {tex}\int\limits_0^4 {\left( {\sqrt x - \frac{x}{2}} \right)} dx \\ {/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{3}\left[ {{x^{3/2}}} \right]_0^4 - \frac{1}{4}\left[ {{x^2}} \right]_0^4 \\ {/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{3}(8) - \frac{1}{4}(16) {/tex} = {tex}\frac{4}{3} \\ {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the ellipse x 2 + 9y 2 = 9 and the straight line x + 3y = 3 is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the ellipse x2 + 9y2 = 9 and the straight line x + 3y = 3 is

questions.question_text: The area between the hyperbola {tex}xy = {c^2}{/tex} , then x – axis and the ordinates at a and b with a > b is :
questions.question_html: The area between the hyperbola {tex}xy = {c^2}{/tex} , then x – axis and the ordinates at a and b with a > b is :
questions.explanation: Required area : = {tex}\int\limits_b^a {\frac{{{c^2}}}{x}} dx {/tex} = {tex}{c^2}\left[ {\log x} \right]_b^a \\ {/tex} = {tex}{c^2}(\log a - \log b) \\ {/tex} = {tex} {c^2}log\left( {\frac{a}{b}} \right) \\ {/tex} . Which is the required solution.

questions.question_text: The area of the smaller portion of the circle x 2 + y 2 = 4 cut off by the line x = 1 is
questions.question_html: The area of the smaller portion of the circle x2 + y2 = 4 cut off by the line x = 1 is
questions.explanation: Required area: = {tex} 2\int\limits_1^2 {\sqrt {4 - {x^2}} dx} \\ {/tex} = {tex}2\left[\frac{x\sqrt{4-x^2}}2+\frac42\sin^{-1}\left(\frac x2\right)\right]_1^2{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\frac42\sin^{-1}\left(1\right)-\frac{\sqrt3}2-\frac42\sin^{-1}\left(\frac12\right)\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{{4\pi - 3\sqrt 3 }}{3} \\{/tex}

questions.question_text: The area of the smaller segment cut off from the circle x 2 + y 2 = 9 by x = 1 is
questions.question_html: The area of the smaller segment cut off from the circle x2 + y2 = 9 by x = 1 is
questions.explanation: Required area: = {tex}2\int\limits_1^3 {\sqrt {9 - {x^2}} dx}{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\frac{x\sqrt{9-x^2}}2+\frac92\sin^{-1}\left(\frac x3\right)\right]_1^3{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\frac92\sin^{-1}\left(1\right)-\frac{2\sqrt2}2-\frac92\sin^{-1}\left(\frac13\right)\right]{/tex} = {tex}9.\frac{\pi }{2} - 2\sqrt 2 - 9.{\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{3}} \right){/tex} = {tex}9\left[ {\frac{\pi }{2} - {{\sin }^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{3}} \right)} \right] - 2\sqrt 2{/tex} = {tex}9{\cos ^{ - 1}}\frac{1}{3} - 2\sqrt 2{/tex} = {tex}9{\sec ^{ - 1}}3 - \sqrt 8 {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area enclosed by the curve {tex}x{y^2} = {\text{ }}{a^2}\left( {a{\text{ }}-{\text{ }}x{\text{ }}} \right){/tex} and the y – axis is
questions.question_html: The area enclosed by the curve {tex}x{y^2} = {\text{ }}{a^2}\left( {a{\text{ }}-{\text{ }}x{\text{ }}} \right){/tex} and the y – axis is
questions.explanation: Required area: = {tex}2\int_0^aa\sqrt{\frac{a-x}x}dx{/tex} {tex}={/tex} {tex}2a\int_0^a\sqrt{\frac{a-x}x}dx{/tex} Put {tex}x=a\sin^2\theta{/tex} , On differentiating, we get {tex}dx=2a\;\sin\theta\;\cos\theta\;d\theta{/tex} For Limits, at {tex}x=0,\;\theta=0{/tex} and at {tex}x=a,\;\theta=\frac{\mathrm\pi}2{/tex} {tex}=2a\int_0^\frac{\mathrm\pi}2\sqrt{\frac{a-a\sin^2\theta}{a\sin^2\theta}}2a\;\sin\theta\;\cos\theta\;d\theta{/tex} {tex}={(2a)^2}\int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {{{\cos }^2}\theta d\theta } {/tex} {tex}= 2{a^2}.\int\limits_0^{\pi /2} {(1 + cos2\theta )d\theta } {/tex} {tex} = 2{a^2}.\left| {\theta + \frac{{\sin 2\theta }}{2}} \right|_0^{\pi /2} {/tex} {tex}= 2{a^2}\left[ {\frac{\pi }{2} + 0 - 0} \right] = \pi {a^2} {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curves {tex}{y^2} = {x^{3\;\;}}and\;\left| y \right| = 2x{/tex} is given by
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curves {tex}{y^2} = {x^{3\;\;}}and\;\left| y \right| = 2x{/tex} is given by
questions.explanation: Both {tex}{y^2} = {x^3}{/tex} and {tex}\left| y \right| = 2x\;{/tex} are symmetric about y – axis and on solving them we get : {tex}x = 0 , \sqrt 2 {/tex} Required area : {tex}2\int\limits_0^{\sqrt 2 } {(2x - {x^3})dx} {/tex} {tex}= 2\left[ {{x^2} - \frac{{{x^4}}}{4}} \right]_0^{\sqrt 2 } = 2sq.units {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curves y 2 = 4x and y = x is equal to
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curves y2 = 4x and y = x is equal to
questions.explanation: The two curves y 2 = 4x and y = x meet where x 2 = 4x i.e ..where x = 0 or x = 4 . Moreover, the parabola lies above the line y = x between x = 0 and x = 4 . Hence, the required area is: {tex}\int\limits_0^4 {\left( {\sqrt {4x} - x} \right)dx} = \int\limits_0^4 {\left( {2{x^{\frac{1}{2}}} - x} \right)} dx {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{{2{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{3/2}} - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_0^4 {/tex} {tex} = \frac{4}{3}\left( {{4^{\frac{3}{2}}}} \right) - \frac{{16}}{2} = \frac{{32}}{3} - 8 = \frac{8}{3} {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area included between the curves {tex}\sqrt x + \;\sqrt {\left| y \right|} = 1{/tex} and {tex}\left| x \right| + \left| y \right| = 1{/tex} is equal to
questions.question_html: The area included between the curves {tex}\sqrt x + \;\sqrt {\left| y \right|} = 1{/tex} and {tex}\left| x \right| + \left| y \right| = 1{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation: Required area : {tex}=2\int\limits_0^1 {(2\sqrt x - 2x)dx} {/tex} {tex}= 2\int\limits_0^1 {(1 - x) - {{(1 - \sqrt x )}^2}} dx{/tex} {tex}= \frac{2}{3}sq.units {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area of the figure bounded by y = e x , y = e -x and the straight line x = 1 is
questions.question_html: The area of the figure bounded by y = ex, y = e-x  and the straight line x = 1 is
questions.explanation: Required area: = {tex}\int\limits_0^1 {({e^x} - {e^{ - x}})dx} = \left[ {{e^x} + {e^{ - x}}} \right]_0^1 = e + \frac{1}{e} - 2 {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area enclosed between the curves y = {tex}\sqrt {x\;\;} ,\;x = 2y + 3\;{/tex} and the x-axis is
questions.question_html: The area enclosed between the curves y = {tex}\sqrt {x\;\;} ,\;x = 2y + 3\;{/tex}and the x-axis is
questions.explanation: The two curves meet where; {tex}\sqrt x = \frac{{x - 3}}{2}{/tex} ...(i) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4x = x 2 - 6x + 9 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 - 10x + 9 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 9,1. Therefore, the two curves meet where x = 9. Therefore, required area: {tex} =\int\limits_0^9 {\sqrt x dx - \int\limits_3^9 {\frac{{x - 3}}{2}dx} } = \frac{2}{3}\left[ {{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}} \right]_0^9 - \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\frac{{{{(x - 3)}^2}}}{2}} \right]_3^9 = 9 \\ {/tex}

questions.question_text: The positive value of the parameter a for which the area of the figure bounded by y = sin ax , y = 0 , x = {tex}\frac{\pi }{a}{/tex} and {tex}x = \frac{{\pi \;}}{{3a}}{/tex} is 3 is equal to
questions.question_html: The positive value of the parameter a for which the area of the figure bounded by y = sin ax , y = 0 , x = {tex}\frac{\pi }{a}{/tex} and {tex}x = \frac{{\pi \;}}{{3a}}{/tex} is 3 is equal to
questions.explanation: We have : {tex}\int\limits_{\frac{\pi }{{3a}}}^{\frac{\pi }{a}} {\sin axdx} = 3 \Rightarrow\left[-\frac{\cos{\displaystyle a}{\displaystyle x}}a\right]_{\textstyle\frac\pi{3a}}^{\textstyle\frac\pi a}=3\Rightarrow \frac{1}{a}\left( {1 + \frac{1}{2}} \right) = 3 \Rightarrow a = \frac{1}{2} \\ {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the parabola y 2 = 4x and the line x + y = 3 is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the parabola y2 = 4x and the line x + y = 3 is
questions.explanation: Required area: = {tex}\int\limits_{ - 6}^2 {\left[ {\frac{{{y^2}}}{4} - (3 - y)} \right]} dy = \left[ {\frac{{{y^3}}}{{12}} - 3y + \frac{{{y^2}}}{2}} \right]_{ - 6}^2 = \frac{{64}}{3} \\ {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curve y = x 2 + 1 and the line x + y = 3 is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curve y = x2 + 1 and the line x + y = 3 is
questions.explanation: The two curves parabola and the line meet where, 3 - x = x 2 + 1 {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} x 2 + x - 2 = 0 {tex}\Leftrightarrow{/tex} x = - 2,1 Therefore, the required area is: {tex} \int\limits_{ - 2}^1 {({y_{line}} - {y_{parabola}})} dx {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_{ - 2}^1 {\left\{ {3 - x - ({x^2} + 1)} \right\}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {2x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - \frac{{{x^3}}}{3}} \right]_{ - 2}^1 = \frac{9}{2} {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area of the region bounded by y = | x - 1| and y = 1 is
questions.question_html: The area of the region bounded by y = | x - 1| and y = 1 is
questions.explanation: Required area : {tex}\left| {\int\limits_0^1 {\left[ {(x - 1) - (1 - x)} \right]} dx} \right| = 1{/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curve {tex}\sqrt x + \sqrt y = 1{/tex} and the coordinate axes is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curve {tex}\sqrt x + \sqrt y = 1{/tex} and the coordinate axes is
questions.explanation: The area bounded by curve and coordinates axis : {tex}\int\limits_0^1 {ydx} = \int\limits_0^1 {{{(1 - \sqrt x )}^2}dx} = \int\limits_0^1 {(1 - 2\sqrt x } + x)dx = 1 - \frac{4}{3} + \frac{1}{2} = \frac{1}{6}sq.units{/tex} .Which is the required solution.

questions.question_text: The area of the region bounded by the curve x 2 = 4y and the straight line x = 4y - 2 is
questions.question_html: The area of the region bounded by the curve x2 = 4y and the straight line x = 4y - 2 is
questions.explanation: Eliminating y , we get : {tex}{x^2} - x - 2 = 0 \Rightarrow x = - 1,2{/tex} Required area : {tex}=\int\limits_{ - 1}^2 {\left( {\frac{x}{4} + \frac{1}{2} - \frac{{{x^2}}}{4}} \right)} dx {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{8}(4 - 1) + \frac{3}{2} - \frac{1}{{12}}(8 + 1) {/tex} {tex}= \frac{3}{8} + \frac{3}{2} - \frac{3}{4} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{9}{8}{/tex} sq.units

questions.question_text: If the area bounded by the curve y 2 = 16 x and the line y = mx is {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} , then m is equal to
questions.question_html: If the area bounded by the curve y2 = 16 x and the line y = mx is {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}, then m is equal to
questions.explanation: {tex}\int_0^\frac{16}{m^2}\left[4\sqrt x-mx\right]dx=\frac23{/tex} {tex}\\\Rightarrow\left[\frac23\left(4x^\frac32\right)-\frac{mx^2}2\right]_0^\frac{16}{m^2}=\frac23{/tex} {tex}\\\Rightarrow\frac83\left(\frac{16}{m^2}\right)^\frac32-\frac m2\left(\frac{16}{m^2}\right)^2=\frac23{/tex} {tex}\\\Rightarrow\frac83\left(\frac{64}{m^3}\right)-\frac{128}{m^3}=\frac23{/tex} {tex}\\\Rightarrow\frac1{m^3}\left(\frac{128}3\right)=\frac23{/tex} {tex}\\\Rightarrow\frac1{m^3}=\frac1{64}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow m=4{/tex}

questions.question_text: The area of the region bunded by the curves y =|x - 2| , x = 3, x = 1 and the x – axis is
questions.question_html: The area of the region bunded by the curves y =|x - 2| , x = 3, x = 1 and the x – axis is
questions.explanation: Required area: {tex}\int\limits_1^3 {ydx} = \int\limits_1^3 {\left| {x - 2} \right|dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_1^2 {\left| {x - 2} \right|dx} + \int\limits_2^3 {\left| {x - 2} \right|dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_1^2 {(2 - x)dx} + \int\limits_2^3 {(x - 2)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {2x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_1^2 + \left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - 2x} \right]_2^3 = 1 {/tex}

questions.question_text: For which of the following values of m, is the area of the region bounded by the curve y = x - x 2 and the line y = mx equal to {tex}\frac{9}{2}{/tex} ?
questions.question_html: For which of the following values of m, is the area of the region bounded by the curve y = x - x2 and the line y = mx equal to {tex}\frac{9}{2}{/tex}?
questions.explanation: Required area: {tex} \int\limits_0^{1 - m} {(x - {x^2} - mx)dx} {/tex} {tex} = \left[ {(1 - m)\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - \frac{{{x^3}}}{3}} \right]_0^{1 - m} {/tex} {tex} = \frac{{{{(1 - m)}^3}}}{2} - \frac{{{{(1 - m)}^3}}}{3} = \frac{9}{2} {/tex} {tex}\\ \Rightarrow \frac{{{{(1 - m)}^3}}}{6} = \frac{9}{2}{/tex} {tex} \\ \Rightarrow \\{/tex} m = - 2

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curve y = x|x|, x-axis and the ordinates x = -1 and x = 1 is given by
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curve y = x|x|, x-axis and the ordinates x = -1 and x = 1 is given by
questions.explanation: Required area : {tex} = \left| {\int\limits_{ - 1}^1 {x\left| x \right|dx} } \right|{/tex} {tex}= \left| {\int\limits_{ - 1}^0 {x\left| x \right|dx} + \int\limits_0^1 {x\left| x \right|dx} } \right| {/tex} {tex}= \left| {\int\limits_{ - 1}^0 { - {x^2}dx} } \right| + \int\limits_0^1 {{x^2}dx} {/tex} {tex}= \left| {\left[ {\frac{{ - {x^3}}}{3}} \right]_{ - 1}^0} \right| - \left[ {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3}} \right]_0^1 {/tex} {tex}= \frac{2}{3}{/tex} sq. units

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curve y = x (x – 1) (x – 2) and the x-axis is equal to
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curve y = x (x – 1) (x – 2) and the x-axis is equal to
questions.explanation: y = f(x) = x(x - 1)(x - 2) is +ve for x > 2, is – ve for 1 < x < 2; +ve for 0 < x < 1 , is – ve for x< 0. Required area: {tex} \int\limits_0^1 {ydx + \left| {\int\limits_1^2 {ydx} } \right|} {/tex} {tex} \int\limits_0^1 {({x^3} - 3{x^2} + 2{x}} )dx + \left| {\int\limits_1^2 {({x^3} - 3{x^2} + 2{x}} )dx} \right|{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{{{x^4}}}{4} - {x^3} + {x^2}} \right]_0^1 + \left| {\left[ {\frac{{{x^4}}}{4} - {x^3} + {x^2}} \right]_1^2} \right| {/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{2}{/tex} sq. units

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curve y = 2x - x 2 and the line x + y = 0 is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curve y = 2x - x2 and the line x + y = 0 is
questions.explanation: The equation y = 2x - x 2 i.e.y - 1 = - (x-1) 2 represents a downward parabola with vertex at (1,1) which meets x – axis where y = 0 .i .e . where x = 0, 2. Also , the line y = - x meets this parabola where – x = 2x - x 2 i.e. where x = 0 , 3. Therefore, required area is: {tex} \int\limits_0^3 {({y_{parabola}} - {y_{line}})dx} = \int\limits_0^3 {(2x - {x^2} - ( - x))dx} = \left[ {\frac{{3{x^2}}}{2} - \frac{{{x^3}}}{3}} \right]_0^3 = \frac{{27}}{2} - 9 = \frac{9}{2}{/tex} sq. units

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curves {tex}y = \sqrt x{/tex} , {tex}2y + 3 = x{/tex} and the x- axis in the first quadrant is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curves {tex}y = \sqrt x{/tex}, {tex}2y + 3 = x{/tex} and the x- axis in the first quadrant is
questions.explanation: To find area the curves y = {tex}\sqrt x {/tex} and x = 2y + 3 and x – axis in the first quadrant., We have ; {tex}{y^2}-{\text{ }}2y{\text{ }}-{\text{ }}3{\text{ }} = {\text{ }}0{/tex} ,( y – 3 ) ( y + 1) = 0 . y = 3 , - 1. In first quadrant , y = 3 and x = 9. Therefore , required area is ; {tex} \int\limits_0^9 {\sqrt x dx - \int\limits_3^9 {\left( {\frac{{x - 3}}{2}} \right)} } = \left[ {\frac{{{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{\frac{3}{2}}}} \right]_0^9 - \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - 3x} \right]_3^9 = 9 \\ {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curves y 2 = 20x and x 2 = 16y is equal to
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curves y2 = 20x and x2 = 16y is equal to
questions.explanation: Eliminating y, we get: {tex}{x^4} = 256 \times 20x{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow x = 0,x = 8{(10)^{\frac{1}{3}}}{/tex} Required area: = {tex}\int\limits_0^{8{{(10)}^{\frac{1}{3}}}} {\left( {\sqrt {20x} - \frac{{{x^2}}}{{16}}} \right)dx} {/tex} {tex} = \frac{{640}}{3} - \frac{{320}}{3} = \frac{{320}}{3}{/tex} sq units

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the parabolas y = (x + 1) 2 ; and ;y = (x - 1) 2 and the line {tex}y = \frac{1}{4}{/tex} is equal to
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the parabolas y = (x + 1)2; and ;y = (x - 1)2 and the line {tex}y = \frac{1}{4}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation: The given curves are y = (x + 1) 2 ...(i) upward parabola with vertex at (-1 ,0) meeting y -axis at (0, 1) y = ( x - 1) 2 ...(ii) upward parabola with vertex at (1 ,0) meeting v-axis at (0,1) y = 1/4 ...(iii) a line parallel to x-axis meeting (i) at {tex}\left(-\frac{1}{2}, \frac{1}{4}\right),\left(-\frac{3}{2}, \frac{1}{4}\right){/tex} and meeting (ii) at {tex}\left(\frac{3}{2}, \frac{1}{4}\right),\left(\frac{1}{2}, \frac{1}{4}\right){/tex} . The graph is as shown The required area is the shaded portion given by AR (BPCQB) = 2 Ar(PQCP) (by symmetry) {tex}=2\left[\int\limits_0^{1 / 2}\left((x-1)^2-\frac{1}{4}\right) d x\right]{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\left(\frac{(x-1)^3}{3}-\frac{x}{4}\right)_0^{1 / 2}\right]{/tex} {tex}=2\left[\left(-\frac{1}{24}-\frac{1}{8}\right)-\left(-\frac{1}{3}\right)\right]{/tex} = {tex}2\left[\frac{-1-3+8}{24}\right]=\frac{1}{3} \text { sq. units. }{/tex}

questions.question_text: The area of the region { ( x , y ) : {tex}{x^2} + {y^2} \leqslant 1 \leqslant x + y\;\} {/tex} is equal to
questions.question_html: The area of the region { ( x , y ) : {tex}{x^2} + {y^2} \leqslant 1 \leqslant x + y\;\} {/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{{\pi - 2}}{4}{/tex} sq. units

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the parabolas y = 5x 2 and y - 9 = 2x 2 is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the parabolas y = 5x2 and y - 9 = 2x2 is
questions.explanation: Required area: {tex}\int\limits_{ - \sqrt 3 }^{\sqrt 3 } {(9 + 2{x^2} - 5{x^2})dx}{/tex} {tex} = 2\int\limits_0^{\sqrt 3 } {(9 - 3{x^2})dx} {/tex} {tex}= 2\left[ {9\sqrt 3 - 3\sqrt 3 } \right] {/tex} {tex}= 12\sqrt 3{/tex} sq.units

questions.question_text: The area enclosed by the parabola y 2 = 2x and its tangents through the point (-2, 0) is
questions.question_html: The area enclosed by the parabola y2 = 2x and its tangents through the point (-2, 0) is
questions.explanation: The tangents are {tex}y = mx + \frac{a}{m},y = mx + \frac{1}{{2m}},{/tex} since {tex}a = \frac{1}{2}{/tex} . It passes through (-2, 0). {tex} \therefore 4{m^2} = 1 \Rightarrow m = \pm \frac{1}{2}{/tex} The tangents are: {tex}y = \frac{x}{2} + 1,y = - \frac{x}{2} - 1{/tex} Required area: {tex} = 2\int\limits_0^3 {\left( {\frac{{{y^2}}}{2} - 2y + 2} \right)dy} {/tex} {tex}= 2\left[ {\frac{4}{3} - 4 + 4} \right] {/tex} {tex} = \frac{8}{3}{/tex} sq.units

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the angle bisectors of the lines {tex}{x^2} - {y^2} + 2y = 1{/tex} and {tex}x + y = 3{/tex} is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the angle bisectors of the lines {tex}{x^2} - {y^2} + 2y = 1{/tex} and {tex}x + y = 3{/tex} is
questions.explanation: The angle bisectors of the line given by {tex}{x^2}-{\text{ }}{y^2} + 2y{\text{ }} = {\text{ }}1{/tex} are x = 0 , y = 1. Required area : = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{\text{ }}.2.2{\text{ }} = {\text{ }}2{\text{ }}sq.{\text{ }}units{/tex} .

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curves y = x 2 and {tex}\;y = \frac{2}{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}}\;{/tex} is equal to
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curves y = x2 and {tex}\;y = \frac{2}{{\left( {1 + {x^2}} \right)}}\;{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation: Required area: = {tex}2\left( {\int\limits_0^1 {\frac{2}{{1 + {x^2}}}} dx - \int\limits_0^1 {{x^2}dx} } \right) {/tex} {tex} = 2\left[ {2{{\tan }^{ - 1}}x} \right]_0^1 - 2\left[ {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3}} \right]_0^1{/tex} {tex} = \pi - \frac{2}{3}{/tex} sq.units

questions.question_text: The area bounded by x = sin t and y = cos t +3 , where -2008 < t < 2008 , is equal to
questions.question_html: The area bounded by x = sin t and y = cos t +3 , where -2008 < t < 2008 , is equal to
questions.explanation: We have : sin t = x , cos t = y – 3 ., therefore , sin {tex}^2{/tex} t + cos {tex}^2{/tex} t = 1, {tex} \Rightarrow {x^2} + {(y - 3)^2} = 1{/tex} It is a circle with centre ( 0, 3 ) and radius is 1. Therefore , area = {tex}\pi {r^2} = \pi \times 1 = \pi {/tex} . Which is the required solution.

questions.question_text: The area enclosed between the curves {tex}{y^2} = x\;and\;y = \left| x \right|{/tex} is
questions.question_html: The area enclosed between the curves {tex}{y^2} = x\;and\;y = \left| x \right|{/tex} is
questions.explanation: Required area : {tex} \int\limits_0^1 {(\sqrt x - x)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{2}{3}{x^{\frac{3}{2}}} - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_0^1 {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{6}sq.units {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area of the plane region bounded by the curves x + y 2 = 0 and x + 3y 2 = 1 is equal to
questions.question_html: The area of the plane region bounded by the curves x + y2 = 0 and x + 3y2 = 1 is equal to
questions.explanation: On solving the given curves, we get y = {tex} \pm {/tex} 1 and x = - 2. Required area: {tex}\left| {\int\limits_{ - 1}^1 {({x_1} - {x_2})dy} } \right| {/tex} {tex} = \left| {\int\limits_{ - 1}^1 {(1 - 3{y^2} + 2{y^2})dy} } \right| {/tex} {tex} = \left| {2\int\limits_0^1 {(1 - {y^2})dy} } \right| {/tex} {tex} = \left| {2\left[ {y - \frac{{{y^3}}}{3}} \right]_0^1} \right|{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex} sq.units

questions.question_text: The area of the region bounded by the parabola (y - 2) 2 = x - 1, the tangent to the parabola at the point (2,3) and the x-axis is equal to
questions.question_html: The area of the region bounded by the parabola (y - 2)2 = x - 1, the tangent to the parabola at the point (2,3) and the x-axis is equal to
questions.explanation: Given parabola is: {tex}{\left( {y - 2} \right)^2} = x - 1 \Rightarrow \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{{2(y - 2)}} {/tex} When y = 3, x = 2 {tex}\; \therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = \frac{1}{2} \\{/tex} Therefore, tangent at ( 2, 3 ) is y – 3 = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} ( x – 2 ). i.e. x – 2y + 4 = 0, therefore required area is: {tex} \int\limits_0^3 {{{(y - 2)}^2} + 1.dy} - \int\limits_0^3 {(2y - 4)dy} = \left[ {\frac{{{{(y - 2)}^3}}}{3} + y} \right]_0^3 - \left[ {{y^2} - 4y} \right]_0^3 = 9 {/tex} sq. units

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curves y = cos x and y = sin x between the ordinates x = 0 and {tex}x = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex} is equal to
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curves y = cos x and y = sin x between the ordinates x = 0 and {tex}x = \frac{\pi }{2}{/tex}is equal to
questions.explanation: Required area = {tex}\int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {\left| {\sin x - \cos x} \right|dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} {(\cos x - \sin x)dx + } \int\limits_{\frac{\pi }{4}}^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {(sinx - \cos x)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\sin x + \cos x} \right]_0^{\frac{\pi }{4}} + \left[ { - cosx - sinx} \right]_{\frac{\pi }{4}}^{\frac{\pi }{2}} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }} + \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }} - (0 + 1) - \left\{ {1 - \left( {\frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }} + \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}} \right)} \right\} {/tex} {tex}= \frac{4}{{\sqrt 2 }} - 2 = 2\sqrt 2 - 2 = 2(\sqrt 2 - 1) {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the parabola y = x 2 and the line y = x is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the parabola y = x2 and the line y = x is
questions.explanation: Required area: {tex}\int\limits_0^1 {(x - {x^2})dx} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - \frac{{{x^3}}}{3}} \right]_0^1{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{6}{/tex} sq.units.

questions.question_text: Let y be the function which passes through (1,2) having slope (2x + 1). The area bounded between the curve and the x-axis is
questions.question_html: Let y be the function which passes through (1,2) having slope (2x + 1). The area bounded between the curve and the x-axis is
questions.explanation: Given slope of the curve is 2x + 1. {tex} \therefore \frac{{dy}}{{dx}} = 2x + 1 \Rightarrow y = {x^2} + x + c {/tex} Also , it passes through (1, 2). {tex}\therefore 2 = 1 + 1 + c \Rightarrow c = 0{/tex} Equation of curve is: y =x 2 +x. Therefore , points of intersection of y = x ( x +1 ) and the x – axis are x = 0 , x = - 1. Required area: {tex}\int\limits_{ - 1}^0 {({x^2} + x)dx}{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\frac{{{x^3}}}{3} + \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_{ - 1}^0{/tex} {tex} = \left| { - \frac{1}{6}} \right| = \frac{1}{6}{/tex} sq. units

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curves {tex}y = \sqrt {5 - {x^2}\;} \;{/tex} and {tex}y = \;\left| {x - 1} \right|{/tex} is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curves {tex}y = \sqrt {5 - {x^2}\;} \;{/tex}and {tex}y = \;\left| {x - 1} \right|{/tex} is
questions.explanation: Required area : {tex}\int\limits_{ - 1}^2 {\sqrt {5 - {x^2}} dx} - \int\limits_{ - 1}^1 {(1 - x)dx} - \int\limits_1^2 {(x - 1)dx} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{{x\sqrt {5 - {x^2}} }}{2} + \frac{5}{2}{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{x}{{\sqrt 5 }}} \right]_{ - 1}^2 {/tex} {tex} - \left[ {x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_{ - 1}^1 - \left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - x} \right]_1^2 \\ {/tex} {tex}= \left( {\frac{{5\pi - 2}}{4}} \right)sq.units {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area of the region bounded by the curves y = |x - 2|, x = 1, x = 3 and the x – axis is
questions.question_html: The area of the region bounded by the curves y = |x - 2|, x = 1, x = 3 and the x – axis is
questions.explanation: Required area is: {tex}\int\limits_1^3 {ydx} = \int\limits_1^3 {\left| {x - 2} \right|dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_1^2 {\left| {x - 2} \right|dx} + \int\limits_2^3 {\left| {x - 2} \right|dx} {/tex} {tex}= \int\limits_1^2 {\left| {2 - x} \right|dx} + \int\limits_2^3 {\left| {x - 2} \right|dx} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {2x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{2}} \right]_1^2 + \left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - 2x} \right]_2^3 = 1 {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area enclosed between the curve {tex}y = {\log _e}(x + e){/tex} and {tex}x = {\log _e}\frac{1}{y}{/tex} and the x- axis is
questions.question_html: The area enclosed between the curve {tex}y = {\log _e}(x + e){/tex} and {tex}x = {\log _e}\frac{1}{y}{/tex} and the x- axis is
questions.explanation: Required area is : {tex} \int\limits_{1 - e}^0 {{{\log }_e}(x + e).dx + \int\limits_0^\infty {{e^{ - x}}dx} } {/tex} {tex} = \left[ {x{{\log }_e}(x - e)} \right]_{1 - e}^0 - \int\limits_{1 - e}^0 {\frac{x}{{x + e}}dx} - \left[ {{e^{ - x}}} \right]_0^\infty {/tex} {tex} = \int\limits_0^{1 - e} {\left( {1 - \frac{e}{{x + e}}} \right)dx} - {e^{ - \infty }} + {e^0} {/tex} {tex} = \left[ {x - e\log (x + e)} \right]_{1 - e}^0 - 0 + 1 = 2 \\{/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by y =| sinx |, the x – axis and the line {tex}\left| x \right| = \pi {/tex} is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by y =| sinx |, the x – axis and the line {tex}\left| x \right| = \pi {/tex} is
questions.explanation: Required area : {tex} = 2\int\limits_0^\pi {\sin xdx} = 2\left[ { - \cos x} \right]_0^\pi = 2[1 + 1] = 4sq.units {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curves {tex}y = \sqrt x,{/tex} 2y + 3 = x and the x-axis in the first quadrant is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curves {tex}y = \sqrt x,{/tex} 2y + 3 = x and the x-axis in the first quadrant is
questions.explanation: Required area: {tex}\int\limits_0^9 {\sqrt x dx} - \int\limits_3^9 {\left( {\frac{{x - 3}}{2}} \right)dx} = \left[ {\frac{{{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{{3/2}}} \right]_0^9 - \frac{1}{2}\left[ {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2} - 3x} \right]_3^9 = 9 \\ {/tex} sq.units

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curve y = logx and y = 2x - 2x 2 is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curve y = logx and y = 2x - 2x2 is
questions.explanation: Required area: {tex}\int\limits_0^1 {\left[ {\left( {2x - 2{x^2}} \right) - \left( {x\log x} \right)} \right]} dx = \left[ {{x^2} - \frac{{2{x^3}}}{3} - \left( {\frac{{{x^2}}}{2}\log x - \frac{{{x^2}}}{4}} \right)} \right]_0^1 = \frac{7}{{12}} {/tex}

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the curves y = |x - 1| and y = 1 is given by
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the curves y = |x - 1| and y = 1 is given by
questions.explanation: The given curves are : (i) y = x – 1 , x > 1 . (ii) y = - ( x – 1 ) , x < 1. (iii) y = 1 these three lines enclose a triangle whose area is : {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} .base.height = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} .2 .1 = 1 sq. unit.

questions.question_text: The area bounded by the lines y = 2 + x , y = 2 – x and x = 2 is
questions.question_html: The area bounded by the lines y = 2 + x , y = 2 – x and x = 2 is
questions.explanation: Reqd. area sq.units {tex}= \int_0^2 {\left( {y - 2} \right)} dy + \int_2^0 {\left( {2 - y} \right)} dy + \int_0^4 2 dy{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\frac{{{y^2}}}{2} - 2y} \right]_0^2 + \left[ {2y - \frac{{{y^2}}}{2}} \right]_2^0 + \left[ {2y} \right]_0^4{/tex} = (2 - 4) - (4 - 2) + 8 = 4

questions.question_text: Ratio of the area cut off from a parabola by any double ordinate is that of the corresponding rectangle contained by that double ordinate and its distance from the vertex is
questions.question_html: Ratio of the area cut off from a parabola by any double ordinate is that of the corresponding rectangle contained by that double ordinate and its distance from the vertex is
questions.explanation: Let y 2 = 4x be a parabola and let x = b be a double ordinate. Then , A 1 = area enclosed by the parabola y 2 = 4ax and the double ordinate x = b. {tex}2\int\limits_0^2 {ydx} = 2\int\limits_0^b {\sqrt {4ax} dx}{/tex} {tex}= 4\sqrt a \int\limits_0^b {\sqrt {{x^3}} dx} {/tex} {tex}= 4\sqrt a \left[ {\frac{2}{3}{x^{\frac{3}{2}}}} \right]_0^b {/tex} {tex}= 4\sqrt a .\frac{2}{3}{b^{\frac{3}{2}}} = \frac{8}{3}{a^{\frac{1}{2}}}{b^{\frac{3}{2}}}{/tex} ....(1) And, A 2 = Area of the rectangle {tex}= 2\sqrt {4ab} .b = 4{a^{\frac{1}{2}}}{b^{\frac{3}{2}}}{/tex} ....(2) Dividing (1) and (2), {tex}{A_1}:{A_2} {/tex} = {tex}\frac{8}{3}{a^{\frac{1}{2}}}{b^{\frac{3}{2}}}:4{a^{\frac{1}{2}}}{b^{\frac{3}{2}}} = 2:3{/tex}

questions.question_text: If the area cut off from a parabola by any double ordinate is k times the corresponding rectangle contained by that double ordinate and its distance from the vertex, then k is equal to
questions.question_html: If the area cut off from a parabola by any double ordinate is k times the corresponding rectangle contained by that double ordinate and its distance from the vertex, then k is equal to
questions.explanation: Required area : = {tex}2\int\limits_0^a {\sqrt {4ax} dx} {/tex} {tex}= k\alpha (2\sqrt {4a\alpha } ){/tex} {tex}= \frac{{8\sqrt a }}{3}{\alpha ^{\frac{3}{2}}}{/tex} {tex} = 4\sqrt a k{\alpha ^{\frac{3}{2}}} \Rightarrow k = \frac{2}{3} {/tex}

questions.question_text: The larger area bounded by {tex}{y^2} = 4x{/tex} and {tex}{x^2} + {y^2} - 2x - 3 = 0\;{/tex} is equal to
questions.question_html: The larger area bounded by {tex}{y^2} = 4x{/tex} and {tex}{x^2} + {y^2} - 2x - 3 = 0\;{/tex}is equal to
questions.explanation: We have , {tex} {x^2} + {y^2} - 2x - 3 = 0 \Rightarrow {(x - 1)^2} + {y^2} = 4 \\{/tex} .It meets y 2 = 4x at x = 1. Required area {tex} = 2\int\limits_3^1 {\sqrt {4x} dx} + {/tex} area of semi-circle with radius 1. = {tex}\frac{8}{3}{/tex} + 2 {tex}\pi {/tex} . sq. units
